steps.observation:
Here is a 3-sentence summary of the text:

The performance of Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) systems depends on hyperparameters such as chunk size and overlap, which balance precision and context coherence. RAG combines a retrieval component with a generative model to fetch relevant passages from an external corpus. The system's behavior can be shaped by prompt templates and instructions in

final_answer: **Chunking strategies used in RAG pipelines include fixed-size chunking, where long texts are split into smaller passages of a fixed size (256-1024 tokens). Overlap matters because it affects context coherence and retrieval precision. Smaller chunks with overlap can improve precision but may lose context.**
